tooltips.dataSpaceOauth2Title: Configure the OAuth 2.0 client for connecting the Data Marketplace to your DISP. Click CONFIGURE to provide a client name and optional description, then click CREATE. Copy the generated Client ID and Client Secret using the buttons provided. These values will be used in the DISP portal under Base Configurations (Base URL: https://demo-api.nxd.foundation/service).
tooltips.orgOauth2Title: Configure the OAuth 2.0 client for connecting your DISP to the Data Marketplace. Click CONFIGURE to create a new client, providing a name and optional description, then click CREATE. Copy the generated Client ID and Client Secret. Return to the Data Marketplace and paste these values under Organisation OAuth 2.0 Client to enable bi-directional communication.
tooltips.holderBaseUrl: The unique URL identifying your organisation’s wallet instance within the DISP environment. Used by the Data Marketplace to send credential offers and receive responses securely.
tooltips.credentialOfferEndpoint: The OpenID4VCI endpoint where your organisation’s wallet receives credential offers from the Data Marketplace. Obtain this from the DISP portal under Digital Wallet (OpenID4VC) → Base Configuration.
tooltips.accessPointEndpoint: The API endpoint that exposes your organisation’s data exchange interface to the Data Marketplace. Obtain this from the DISP portal under Data Marketplace → Base Configuration.
tooltips.softwareStatement: A digitally signed credential issued by the Data Marketplace once both endpoints are configured. It proves that your business wallet is authorised and trusted for communication with the Data Marketplace. You can review and accept this statement in the DISP portal under Digital Wallet (OpenID4VC) → Wallet Unit (Holder).

knowledgeHub.title: CRANE d-HDSI Knowledge Hub
knowledgeHub.subtitle: Learn about the CRANE d-HDSI Data Marketplace, data consent management, and the tools available to you.

consent.title: Consent & Data Rights
consent.subtitle: Understand how data consent works in CRANE and your rights as a participant.
consent.whatIsMarketplace: What is the CRANE Data Marketplace?
consent.whatIsMarketplaceContent: [CRANE](https://crane-pcp.eu/) is an H2020 Pre-Commercial Procurement European project that aims to develop an integrated self-management model to improve chronic patient's wellbeing through the European Health Data Space concept implementation. The CRANE Data Marketplace serves as a secure digital ecosystem where organisations can publish, discover, and exchange data services under a shared governance framework. It acts as a directory service of governed Data Sources within the CRANE health data ecosystem. Unlike traditional data-sharing arrangements, it provides a structured environment where multiple organisations, including healthcare providers, social care systems, and the welfare tech industry, can participate and enter into bilateral agreements dynamically in a trusted and governed manner.
consent.whatIsDAContent: A Data Agreement specifies the precise terms governing how data can be utilised and shared between an individual and an organisation. Often formulated based on a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA), it creates a clear contractual relationship that protects both data providers and consumers whilst enabling legitimate data flows.
consent.whatIsDDA: What is a Data Disclosure Agreement?
consent.whatIsDDAContent: Derived from a Data Agreement, the Data Disclosure Agreement (DDA) formalises the specific conditions under which data is made available and accessed between two organisations. It provides transparency on data characteristics, quality, and permissible use cases before any exchange occurs.
consent.dexaFrameworkContent:
The diagram below illustrates the Data Exchange Agreements (DEXA) landscape that underpins the [CRANE](https://crane-pcp.eu/) Data Marketplace. It shows how the different agreement types work together to enable trusted, governed data exchange across the ecosystem.

At the centre is the **Individual** (citizen or patient), connected to organisations through **Data Agreements** that define the terms of data usage. **Organisation A** (the Data Source / Issuer / Data Producer) and **Organisation B** (the Data Using Service / Verifier / Data Consumer) are linked by a **Data Disclosure Agreement** that governs organisation-to-organisation data sharing. A **Data Processing Agreement** connects the data Supplier to Organisation A.

Above the individual, a **Delegate** can act on their behalf through a **Delegation Agreement**, supporting caregiver scenarios. Each organisation holds a **Business Wallet** for cryptographic identity verification using eIDAS 2.0, while the Delegate uses an **EUDI Wallet**.

This framework ensures that every data transaction, whether between an individual and an organisation, or between two organisations, is governed by explicit, auditable agreements aligned with GDPR and the European Health Data Space concept.
consent.trustAndVerificationContent: All ecosystem data transactions leverage eIDAS 2.0-based Business Wallets to provide cryptographic verification of each organisation's identity (via Legal Person Identification Data). This technical infrastructure ensures that all marketplace participants are who they claim to be, creating a foundation of trust essential for secure data exchange. As [CRANE](https://crane-pcp.eu/) aims to build trust with patients through transparent and open policies, the use of verifiable credentials and decentralised identity ensures that data sharing is both secure and auditable.
consent.citizenRightsIntro: A core goal of [CRANE](https://crane-pcp.eu/) is to help citizens move from being passive patients to active, empowered participants in their own health by giving them access, new insights, and control of their own data through GDPR legislation and the European data strategy. As a citizen within the CRANE d-HDSI ecosystem, you have full control over how your personal health data is collected, processed, and shared. Every organisation participating in the ecosystem integrates two key modules into their applications to ensure transparency and empower you to exercise your rights under [GDPR](https://docs.igrant.io/regulations/reg-eu-gdpr/).
consent.privacyDashboardContent:
The Privacy Dashboard is a data rights management interface integrated into the citizen's mobile application. Built on ISO standards (ISO 27560), it provides a clear overview of every data processing activity involving your personal data.

As shown in the screenshot, the Privacy Dashboard presents an Overview section with contact details for the Data Protection Officer, followed by a list of all **Data Agreements** associated with your account. Each agreement represents a specific dataset, for example Continuous Glucose Monitoring (CGM), User Registration Data, Insulin Usage, Research Use of Data, or Contractual Purpose. Beside each agreement is a toggle switch showing the current status: **Allow** (green) or **Disallow** (grey). You can tap any agreement to view its full details or tap the toggle to change your consent preference in real time.

Through the Privacy Dashboard, you can:

- View all data agreements regardless of their lawful basis under [GDPR Article 6](https://docs.igrant.io/regulations/reg-eu-gdpr/#article-6-lawfulness-of-processing), whether consent, legitimate interest, contractual necessity, legal obligation, or public interest
- See exactly which datasets are being collected and for what purposes, fulfilling the transparency requirements of [GDPR Article 5(1)(a)](https://docs.igrant.io/regulations/reg-eu-gdpr/#article-5-principles-relating-to-processing-of-personal-data)
- Exercise your rights by allowing or disallowing data processing for each agreement
- Monitor how your data is being consumed at any given time across all ecosystem participants
- Contact the Data Protection Officer for any queries about data management
consent.consentModuleContent:
When data is shared between CRANE d-HDSI ecosystem participants, the Consent Module activates to ensure you are fully informed before any data exchange occurs.

The Consent Module presents:

- The full details of the [Data Agreement](https://docs.igrant.io/concepts/data-agreements/), including what personal data attributes will be shared
- The lawful basis for processing under [Article 6](https://docs.igrant.io/regulations/reg-eu-gdpr/#article-6-lawfulness-of-processing) (e.g., consent, legitimate interest, contractual purpose)
- Data retention periods, geographic restrictions, and storage locations
- The identity of the data controller and any receiving organisations
- Policy URLs and jurisdiction information

You can review all data attributes that will be exchanged before giving or withdrawing consent, in accordance with [Article 7](https://docs.igrant.io/regulations/reg-eu-gdpr/#article-7-conditions-for-consent). This ensures every data sharing transaction is transparent and under your control.
consent.gdprRightsContent:
The CRANE d-HDSI ecosystem is built on the foundational principles of [GDPR Article 5](https://docs.igrant.io/regulations/reg-eu-gdpr/#article-5-principles-relating-to-processing-of-personal-data), which requires that personal data is processed lawfully, fairly, and transparently. All data processing within the ecosystem must have a valid lawful basis under [GDPR Article 6](https://docs.igrant.io/regulations/reg-eu-gdpr/#article-6-lawfulness-of-processing), whether that is consent, contractual necessity, legitimate interest, or another legal ground.

Through the Privacy Dashboard and Consent Module, you can exercise the following rights:

- **[Right of Access (Article 15)](https://docs.igrant.io/regulations/reg-eu-gdpr/#article-15-right-of-access-by-the-data-subject)**: View all data being processed about you through the Privacy Dashboard, including the purposes and categories of data involved
- **[Right to Rectification (Article 16)](https://docs.igrant.io/regulations/reg-eu-gdpr/#article-16-right-to-rectification)**: Request corrections to inaccurate personal data held by any ecosystem participant
- **[Right to Erasure (Article 17)](https://docs.igrant.io/regulations/reg-eu-gdpr/#article-17-right-to-erasure-right-to-be-forgotten)**: Request deletion of your personal data when it is no longer necessary for the original purpose (right to be forgotten)
- **[Right to Restriction (Article 18)](https://docs.igrant.io/regulations/reg-eu-gdpr/#article-18-right-to-restriction-of-processing)**: Limit how your data is processed by toggling data agreements to 'Disallow' in the Privacy Dashboard
- **[Right to Data Portability (Article 20)](https://docs.igrant.io/regulations/reg-eu-gdpr/#article-20-right-to-data-portability)**: Receive your data in a structured, commonly used, and machine-readable format
- **[Right to Object (Article 21)](https://docs.igrant.io/regulations/reg-eu-gdpr/#article-21-right-to-object)**: Object to data processing, particularly where processing is based on legitimate interest or used for research purposes
- **[Right to Withdraw Consent (Article 7(3))](https://docs.igrant.io/regulations/reg-eu-gdpr/#article-7-conditions-for-consent)**: Withdraw your consent at any time through the Privacy Dashboard toggle, as easily as it was given

These rights are built directly into the ecosystem's technical infrastructure, making them easy to exercise without complex legal procedures. The Privacy Dashboard provides a unified view across all participating organisations, ensuring full transparency.
consent.caregiverRightsContent: Content coming soon. This section will explain how caregivers can act on behalf of individuals they support.

guides.title: User Guides
guides.subtitle: Step-by-step guides for each type of CRANE user.

provider.title: Organisation / Service Provider Guide
provider.subtitle: Complete guide for organisations joining the CRANE Data Marketplace.
provider.prerequisitesContent:
Before you can join the Data Marketplace, your organisation must meet one of these conditions:

1. You are already onboarded with a Data Intermediation Service Provider (DISP). The DISP provides consent management and digital wallet services.
2. You have implemented equivalent functions (adopting consent management and digital wallets) that meet the governance requirements.
provider.createAccountContent: Visit the Data Marketplace onboarding page to register your organisation. You will need to provide the organisation administrator's details (name, email, password) and your organisation's details (name, description, sector, location, policy URL).
provider.connectWalletContent: After account creation, provide the Business Wallet Address obtained from your DISP account. The Business Wallet Address can be copied from the Base Configurations page under Digital Wallet (OpenID4VC) in the DISP portal.
provider.verifyOrgContent: Once the organisation wallet is connected, the Data Marketplace will redirect to the wallet and request access to your Legal Person Identification Data (Legal PID) to verify the organisation's legitimacy. Click Confirm to approve.
provider.acceptCoCContent: The organisation administrator is presented with the Data Marketplace Code of Conduct. Review the document carefully, then click Sign and Continue to proceed. Once signed, the organisation is successfully onboarded.
provider.configureWalletOAuthContent:
To enable secure, bi-directional communication between the DISP and the Data Marketplace, configure the organisation wallet and OAuth 2.0 clients through the Developer APIs page. You will need to:

1. Configure the Organisation Wallet with Credential Offer Endpoint and Access Point Endpoint.
2. Request a Software Statement credential to your business wallet.
3. Create a Data Marketplace OAuth 2.0 client and configure it in your DISP.
4. Create a DISP OAuth 2.0 client and configure it in the Data Marketplace.
provider.publishDDAsContent: Create DDAs by linking existing Data Agreements within your DISP platform. Once published, each DDA appears on the Data Marketplace in an unlisted state. You can then request a review, and upon approval by the Review Committee, list the agreement for discovery by other organisations.
provider.signDDAsContent: Explore available data sources on the public discovery page. Review published DDAs and their associated APIs. To sign an agreement, log in and click 'Sign with Business Wallet'. The signed copy is transmitted back to the Data Source and recorded in your Signed Agreements audit trail.

citizen.title: Citizen Guide
citizen.subtitle: Learn how to use the MyHealthEnabler app to manage your health data, control who can access it, and keep your personal information safe.
citizen.introContent:
MyHealthEnabler is a mobile app designed for patients and citizens who want to stay in control of their personal health data. It is part of the [CRANE](https://crane-pcp.eu/) project, which helps people in rural areas across Europe manage chronic health conditions more effectively.

With MyHealthEnabler, you can:

- **See exactly who has access to your data** and what they use it for
- **Give or withdraw your permission** for organisations to use your health information
- **Store your digital identity securely** so you can prove who you are when needed

The app puts you in charge of your own health data, giving you a clear picture of how it is being used at all times.
citizen.gettingStartedContent:
To begin using MyHealthEnabler:

- Download the app on your mobile device (available for both Android and iOS)
- Create your account by registering with your personal details
- Once registered, you will be connected to the healthcare organisations that participate in your region
citizen.privacyDashboardContent:
The Privacy Dashboard is available from within the MyHealthEnabler app settings. It shows you all the data agreements linked to your account.

Each agreement represents a specific type of data that an organisation collects or handles on your behalf. For example:

- **Continuous Glucose Monitoring (CGM) Dataset**: Health readings from your glucose monitoring device
- **User Registration Data**: Your basic account information
- **Insulin Usage Dataset**: Records of your insulin dosage and usage
- **Research Use of Data**: Information shared for approved medical research
- **Contractual Purpose**: Data needed to provide you with healthcare services

Each agreement has a simple toggle switch: **Allow** (green) means the organisation can use that data, and **Disallow** (grey) means you have opted out. You can change your choice at any time by tapping the toggle. Tap on any agreement to see its full details, including why the data is being used, how long it is kept, and which organisation is responsible for it.
citizen.consentContent:
When an organisation asks to access your data for the first time, MyHealthEnabler shows you the full details before anything is shared. You can review:

- What specific information will be collected or shared
- Why it is being used
- How long it will be stored
- Which organisation is responsible for looking after it
- Where the data will be kept

You can choose to **Authorise** or **Cancel** the request. If you authorise, your permission is recorded and the data sharing begins. You can change your mind at any time by going back to the Privacy Dashboard and switching the agreement to Disallow.
citizen.walletContent:
MyHealthEnabler includes a built-in digital identity wallet that keeps your personal credentials safe on your device. The wallet allows you to:

- **Receive official documents** from trusted organisations, such as proof of identity or health-related certificates
- **Share only what is needed** when a service asks to verify your identity, without revealing unnecessary personal details
- **Stay connected** with healthcare providers and other organisations you interact with
- **Respond to requests** through notifications when an organisation needs to check your identity
- **Back up and restore** your wallet data securely

Your identity wallet ensures that your personal information stays under your control and is shared only when you choose to share it.
citizen.dataFlowContent:
When you use MyHealthEnabler, your data is only ever shared in a way that you can see and control:

- Your health data (for example, from glucose monitors or other devices) is collected by the app
- Before any data is shared with an organisation, the app asks for your clear permission
- Once you give permission, data is shared securely with the organisations you have approved
- You can check all your active agreements and their status through the Privacy Dashboard at any time
- Every action is recorded, so there is always a clear trail of what was shared and when
citizen.dpoContent: The Privacy Dashboard includes contact details for the Data Protection Officer (DPO) of each organisation. If you have any questions about how your data is being looked after, used, or shared, you can get in touch with the DPO directly using the contact information in the app.

carer.title: Caregiver Guide
carer.subtitle: A guide for caregivers supporting individuals with their data management.
carer.comingSoon: Content coming soon. This guide will cover delegated access, supporting individuals with consent management, and caregiver-specific tools.

tools.title: Tools & Developer Resources
tools.subtitle: Technical documentation and resources for integrating with the CRANE ecosystem.
tools.consentSDKContent: The Consent Management SDK enables developers to integrate the Privacy Dashboard into mobile applications, providing end-users with a transparent interface to view, manage, and update their consent preferences in real-time. Key functions include displaying data agreements and consent policies, updating consent status, listening for consent status changes, supporting localisation, and managing individuals.
tools.walletSDKContent: The Data Wallet SDK enables Android and iOS applications to embed wallet capabilities for managing verifiable credentials and establishing connections with issuer or verifier organisations. Key functions include initialising and displaying the wallet UI, managing verifiable credentials and connections, handling deep links and notifications for credential issuance and verification, and enabling secure backup and restore.
tools.restAPIContent: Comprehensive reference for the developer APIs used to interact with the Data Marketplace and DISP services.
tools.tokenValidationContent: Once a DDA has been signed, the Data Using Service can begin retrieving data via the DISP. Every incoming API request must undergo token validation and authorisation. The validation process ensures the request originates from a verified Data Using Service, the access token is valid and unexpired, and the requested API endpoint is explicitly permitted under the associated Data Agreement.
tools.resourceServerContent: A reference implementation for Data Source organisations demonstrating how to set up a Resource Server protected using the Authorisation Server provided by the DISP.
tools.mobileAppContent: A reference mobile application demonstrating practical integration of both the Data Wallet and Consent Management SDKs, serving as a template for building user-facing health data applications.
tools.marketplaceAdminContent: Oversees the entire ecosystem, managing data service listings, enforcing governance policies, and ensuring regulatory compliance across all marketplace activities.
tools.orgAdminContent: Represents individual participating organisations, publishes data services to the marketplace, and negotiates agreements with other verified organisations to enable data exchange.

faq.title: Troubleshooting & FAQ
faq.subtitle: Find answers to common questions and resolve issues.
faq.a_cantCreateAccount: Ensure all required fields are filled (admin name, email, password, organisation name, sector, location). Your password must be 8-20 characters. If your email is already registered, try logging in or use a different email.
faq.q_continueOnboarding: I started onboarding but didn't finish. How do I continue?
faq.a_continueOnboarding: After account creation you can log in anytime to continue onboarding. The system will redirect you to the next incomplete step (wallet connection, verification, or Code of Conduct signing).
faq.q_walletNotFound: Where do I find my Business Wallet Address?
faq.a_walletNotFound: Your Business Wallet Address is available in your DISP portal under Digital Wallet (OpenID4VC) > Base Configuration. Copy the full URL and paste it in the Data Marketplace.
faq.a_walletConnectionFails: Verify that the Business Wallet Address is correct and that your DISP account is active. Ensure your browser allows redirects. If the issue persists, contact your DISP provider.
faq.a_verificationFails: Ensure your organisation's Legal PID credential is properly issued in your DISP wallet. Check that the credential has not expired. Try clearing your browser cache and attempting verification again.
faq.q_softwareStatement: My Software Statement is not appearing
faq.a_softwareStatement: After requesting the Software Statement, check your DISP portal under Digital Wallet (OpenID4VC) > Wallet Unit (Holder). You need to click 'Click to get the credential issued' and then Accept. The statement will then appear in both the DISP and the Data Marketplace.
faq.q_oauthConfusion: I'm confused about which OAuth 2.0 client goes where
faq.a_oauthConfusion:
There are two OAuth 2.0 clients to configure:

1. Data Marketplace OAuth 2.0 Client: Created in the Data Marketplace, its Client ID and Secret are pasted into your DISP portal (Data Marketplace > Base Configurations).
2. Organisation OAuth 2.0 Client: Created in your DISP portal, its Client ID and Secret are pasted into the Data Marketplace (Developer APIs > Organisation OAuth 2.0 Client).
faq.q_ddaUnlisted: My DDA is stuck in 'Unlisted' status
faq.a_ddaUnlisted: After publishing a DDA from your DISP, it appears as Unlisted in the Data Marketplace. You need to click 'Request Review' to submit it for governance review. The Review Committee will then approve or reject it.
faq.q_ddaNotVisible: My approved DDA is not visible on the public page
faq.a_ddaNotVisible: After the DDA is approved, you still need to click 'List' to make it discoverable on the public discovery page. Only listed DDAs are visible to other organisations.
faq.a_tokenErrors: Ensure your access token has not expired. Verify that the requested API endpoint and HTTP method match those authorised in the Data Agreement linked to the DDA. Check that your DISP's Authorisation Endpoint is correctly configured.
faq.contactSupportContent: If you cannot resolve your issue, please contact support at support@igrant.io with a description of the problem and any error messages you see.
